title: teucrium scordium subsp. serratum
intro: Teucrium scordium subsp. serratum is a subspecies of the Teucrium scordium plant, also known as the Saxifrage Germander, a type of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Teucrium scordium subsp. serratum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
